words.def: a careful study to find new facts or understand something better
words.ex: After months of research, the scientist discovered that cats sleep 16 hours a day — something cat owners already knew.
frames: Extensive research has been conducted on ___.

words.def: the natural world around us, or the conditions that affect how you live or work
words.ex: The classroom environment was so cold that students wore jackets, scarves, and angry faces.
frames: The ___ has a significant impact on the environment.
In order to protect the environment, ___.
The learning environment plays a crucial role in ___.

words.def: a series of steps or actions done to reach a result
words.ex: The process of making coffee is simple: boil water, add coffee, pretend you're not tired.
frames: The process of ___ involves several stages.
This is a natural process that occurs when ___.
In order to speed up the process, ___.

words.def: to be different from each other, or to change
words.ex: Exam scores vary from student to student — some get 9, some get 4, and some get creative excuses.
frames: There is a wide variety of ___.
Opinions vary considerably on the topic of ___.

words.def: an idea that tries to explain why something happens or how something works
words.ex: In theory, studying every day should improve your score. In practice, Netflix keeps getting in the way.

words.def: to plan and make something, or the way something is planned and made
words.ex: The building was designed to look like a boat, but most people thought it looked like a shoe.

words.def: to include something as a necessary part, or to make someone take part
words.ex: The job involves answering phones, making coffee, and pretending to look busy.
frames: This would involve making changes to ___.

words.def: machines, tools, and scientific knowledge used to solve problems or do things
words.ex: Thanks to modern technology, you can video-call your grandmother — and she can accidentally hang up on you.
frames: Modern technology has made it possible to ___.
Advances in technology have led to ___.
The use of technology in ___ has increased significantly.

words.def: to succeed in doing something, especially after a lot of effort
words.ex: She achieved the highest score in the class — by studying every night while her friends watched TV.
frames: In order to achieve ___, it is necessary to ___.
This is a remarkable achievement because ___.
The goal of ___ was finally achieved when ___.

words.def: a way of dealing with a problem or situation
words.ex: His approach to cooking was unique: open the fridge, look inside, close the fridge, order pizza.
frames: A different approach to ___ would be to ___.
The most effective approach is to ___.
This approach has several advantages, including ___.

words.def: facts or signs that show something is true or exists
words.ex: The evidence was clear: muddy footprints, an open cookie jar, and a guilty-looking dog.

words.def: important enough to notice or have an effect
words.ex: There was a significant increase in ice cream sales — probably because summer arrived.
frames: This has a significant impact on ___.
There is a significant difference between ___ and ___.

words.def: a good result or advantage that comes from something
words.ex: One benefit of learning English is that you can understand song lyrics — and realise they make no sense.
frames: One of the main benefits of ___ is ___.

words.def: to need something, or to say that something must be done
words.ex: The exam requires a pencil, an eraser, and a strong desire to survive.
frames: This requires a great deal of ___.
One of the basic requirements is ___.

words.def: a separate part of something larger
words.ex: The reading section of IELTS has three passages — each one longer and harder than the last.
frames: As mentioned in the previous section, ___.

words.def: very important or very large
words.ex: A major problem with alarm clocks is that they work exactly when you don't want them to.
frames: The majority of people believe that ___.
This plays a major role in ___.

words.def: to think about something carefully before deciding
words.ex: Before answering, consider this: is the question asking what you think, or what the passage says?
frames: A considerable amount of ___ is needed to ___.
When considering ___, it is important to ___.

words.def: to build something, or to put parts together to make something
words.ex: The bridge was constructed in only two years, which is faster than most people finish their homework.
frames: The construction of ___ took several years.

words.def: to develop and change slowly over a long time
words.ex: Languages evolve over time — that's why your grandparents don't understand your text messages.
frames: The theory of evolution explains how ___.
This is a rapidly evolving field because ___.

words.def: to move people or things from one place to another, or the system used to do this
words.ex: Public transport in big cities can be fast, cheap, and crowded — pick any two.
frames: The transport system needs to be improved because ___.

words.def: the system of making, buying, and selling things in a country or area
words.ex: The economy grew so fast that even the pigeons in the city looked richer.
frames: The ___ has a significant effect on the economy.
In order to boost the economy, governments should ___.

words.def: a part of a place, city, or country, or a subject or topic
words.ex: This area of the city is famous for two things: great food and terrible parking.
frames: This is a key area of concern because ___.
One important area of research is ___.

words.def: to answer or react to something
words.ex: When asked about his grades, he responded with silence — which was also a kind of answer.
frames: In response to ___, several changes were made.

words.def: a strong effect or influence on something
words.ex: Social media has had a huge impact on how teenagers communicate — mostly through memes.
frames: This has a significant impact on ___.
The impact of ___ on ___ cannot be ignored.
In order to reduce the negative impact, ___.

words.def: a particular way or method of doing something, especially one that needs skill
words.ex: Her study technique was simple but effective: read, cover, write, check, repeat.
frames: One effective technique for ___ is to ___.

words.def: a group of people who work together to do something
words.ex: A good team is like a good sandwich — every part matters, and one bad ingredient ruins everything.
frames: Members of the team were responsible for ___.

words.def: where something comes from, or a person or thing that gives information
words.ex: The internet is a great source of information — and also a great source of distraction.

words.def: a group of different things of the same general type, or the difference between highest and lowest
words.ex: The shop offers a wide range of products — from things you need to things you definitely don't need.
frames: A wide range of ___ is available.
The study covered a broad range of ___.

words.def: the power to do work, or the strength and effort you use to do things
words.ex: Solar energy comes from the sun. Student energy comes from coffee.
frames: Renewable energy sources such as ___ are becoming more common.
Energy consumption has increased due to ___.

words.def: almost the same, but not exactly the same
words.ex: Twins may look similar, but their personalities can be completely different — one likes maths, the other likes sleeping.
frames: This is similar to ___ in several ways.
There are remarkable similarities between ___ and ___.

words.def: facts and information, especially numbers, collected for study
words.ex: The data showed that students who eat breakfast score higher — finally, a reason to eat pancakes.

words.def: a large area of land, usually part of a country or the world
words.ex: Different regions of the country have different weather — and different opinions about what counts as 'cold'.
frames: There are significant regional differences in ___.

words.def: possible in the future, or the ability to develop into something
words.ex: Every student has the potential to succeed — some just need more coffee and less Netflix.
frames: There are potential risks associated with ___.
In order to reach their full potential, students need ___.

words.def: to continue to live or exist, especially after a difficult or dangerous situation
words.ex: Some plants can survive without water for weeks. Some students can survive without sleep for days.

words.def: the job or purpose that someone or something has in a situation
words.ex: Teachers play an important role in students' lives — even when the role is 'person who gives homework'.
frames: Education plays a vital role in ___.

words.def: to help make something happen, or to give something to help
words.ex: Everyone contributed to the group project — one person did the work, and four contributed their names.
frames: ___ has made a significant contribution to ___.
This contributes to the problem of ___.

words.def: to study something carefully to understand it or find out what it is made of
words.ex: Scientists analysed the water sample and found it contained H2O — which surprised nobody.
frames: A detailed analysis of ___ revealed that ___.

words.def: a length of time
words.ex: Over a period of three months, he learned 500 new words — and forgot where he put his keys.
frames: Over a period of ___, significant changes occurred.

words.def: to recognise something or discover exactly what it is
words.ex: The study identified three main reasons why students fail: no sleep, no notes, no idea.
frames: The study identified ___ as a key factor.
Several problems have been identified, including ___.

words.def: relating to the body, or to things you can touch and see
words.ex: Physical exercise is good for you, but physical exercise at 6am is a form of punishment.
frames: Regular physical activity can help to ___.
Both physical and mental health are important for ___.

words.def: an important or interesting part of something
words.ex: The main feature of this phone is the camera. The main feature of my old phone was that it survived being dropped 47 times.
frames: A distinctive feature of ___ is that ___.

words.def: to show or point to something, or to suggest that something is true
words.ex: The results indicate that chocolate makes people happy — a fact that required no research at all.
frames: This indicates a significant change in ___.

words.def: a plan or method for achieving something, especially over a long time
words.ex: His exam strategy was to answer the easy questions first and leave the hard ones for his future self.
frames: One effective strategy for ___ is to ___.
In order to develop a strategy, it is necessary to ___.
The government adopted a new strategy to ___.

words.def: to make something new that did not exist before
words.ex: The artist created a painting so beautiful that even the museum's security guard stopped to look.
frames: In order to create ___,  it is important to ___.
The creation of ___ has led to ___.

words.def: a planned way of doing something
words.ex: There are many methods of studying, but staring at your textbook while thinking about lunch is not one of them.
frames: The most effective method of ___ is ___.

words.def: knowing that something exists or is happening
words.ex: Most people are aware that exercise is healthy. Fewer people are aware that their gym membership is unused.
frames: People are becoming increasingly aware of ___.
It is important to raise awareness about ___.

words.def: to make something known that was hidden or secret
words.ex: The research revealed something surprising: people who read more also sleep better. (The book falls on their face.)
frames: This reveals a significant difference between ___ and ___.

words.def: to need something or someone, or to trust that they will help
words.ex: Students rely on teachers for knowledge, parents for food, and wifi for survival.
frames: This is a reliable method for ___.

words.def: to say what you think will happen in the future
words.ex: Scientists can predict the weather for next week, but nobody can predict what teenagers will do next.

words.def: one single person, separate from others in a group
words.ex: Each individual has different strengths — some are good at maths, others are good at avoiding maths.
frames: Individual differences play a role in ___.
The needs of the individual should be ___.

words.def: to happen, especially without being planned
words.ex: Earthquakes occur without warning. So do pop quizzes.
frames: Changes occur as a result of ___.

words.def: a belief or way of doing something that has existed for a long time
words.ex: In some traditions, the oldest person eats first. In student tradition, the fastest person eats first.
frames: There is a long tradition of ___ in this country.
Traditional methods of ___ are being replaced by ___.

words.def: a planned piece of work that has a clear purpose and takes time to finish
words.ex: The construction project was supposed to take one year. It took three. Nobody was surprised.
frames: A major project was launched to ___.
It is projected that ___ will increase by ___.

words.def: a place where something is, was, or will be built or located
words.ex: The construction site was so noisy that even the birds wore tiny hard hats and complained.

words.def: something difficult that tests your ability or determination
words.ex: Learning 500 new words is a challenge. Remembering them all on exam day is a miracle.
frames: One of the biggest challenges is ___.
In order to overcome this challenge, ___.

words.def: the way the parts of something are put together or organised
words.ex: A good essay has a clear structure: introduction, body, conclusion. A bad essay has: panic, panic, panic.

words.def: made of many different parts and difficult to understand
words.ex: The human brain is the most complex organ — which explains why understanding other people is so hard.
frames: This is a highly complex issue because ___.
The complexity of ___ makes it difficult to ___.

words.def: one of the things that affects a situation or causes something to happen
words.ex: Many factors affect exam results, including study time, sleep, and whether Mercury is in retrograde.
frames: An important factor to consider is ___.

words.def: to change to fit a new situation or condition
words.ex: Humans can adapt to almost anything — extreme cold, extreme heat, and extremely slow wifi.
frames: Animals must adapt to ___ in order to survive.
The ability to adapt to ___ is essential for ___.
This is an adaptation that helps ___ to ___.

words.def: to give special attention to one thing, or the centre of attention
words.ex: The teacher told us to focus on the lesson, but my brain focused on what to eat for lunch.
frames: It is important to focus on ___ rather than ___.

words.def: to keep something in good condition or make sure it continues
words.ex: It is important to maintain your health — but maintaining a diet while your friend eats pizza is truly heroic.
frames: In order to maintain ___, it is necessary to ___.
The building has been well maintained since ___.

words.def: relating to the whole world
words.ex: Climate change is a global problem — unfortunately, there is no global remote control to fix it.
frames: ___ is a global issue that affects ___.

words.def: able to be used or obtained
words.ex: Free Wi-Fi is available at the café — but a free table is a completely different story.
frames: This information is readily available to ___.
The resources available to ___ are limited.

words.def: to share information, ideas, or feelings with others
words.ex: Teenagers communicate with their parents using three words: fine, nothing, and whatever.
frames: A lack of communication can lead to ___.

words.def: right or suitable for a particular situation
words.ex: Wearing pyjamas is appropriate for sleeping — but apparently not for job interviews.
frames: The most appropriate response would be to ___.
This may not be appropriate for ___.

words.def: a piece of work that needs to be done
words.ex: The teacher gave us a simple task: write 500 words. The hard part was making 50 words look like 500.
frames: Workers were given the task of ___.

words.def: to notice, think of, or understand something in a particular way
words.ex: The students perceived the test as unfair — mainly because it had questions on it.

words.def: to make certain that something happens
words.ex: To ensure you pass the exam, study every day. To ensure you fail, study only on the bus ride there.
frames: Steps must be taken to ensure ___.
This is designed to ensure that ___.
In order to ensure ___, it is important to ___.

words.def: to believe something is true without having proof
words.ex: Never assume the exam will be easy — the exam has feelings too, and it will prove you wrong.
frames: The assumption that ___ may be incorrect.

words.def: bad or harmful; the opposite of positive
words.ex: Social media can have a negative effect on sleep — especially when you check it at 3 AM.
frames: ___ can have a negative impact on ___.
This may lead to negative consequences such as ___.

words.def: to control something by making rules or laws
words.ex: The government regulates how much sugar goes in drinks — but nobody regulates how much sugar goes in your mouth.
frames: There is a need for stricter regulation of ___.

words.def: clearly defined or relating to one particular thing
words.ex: The doctor gave specific instructions: rest, drink water, and stop Googling your symptoms.
frames: One specific example of this is ___.

words.def: relating to chemistry, or a substance made by or used in chemistry
words.ex: The chemical reaction in the experiment was impressive — the chemical reaction from the teacher when it exploded was even more impressive.

words.def: to become smaller, fewer, or less; or a decrease in something
words.ex: The number of students who enjoy homework has been in steady decline — from three to zero.
frames: There has been a significant decline in ___.
The population has declined by ___ percent.
This led to a gradual decline in ___.

words.def: able to continue for a long time without damaging the environment
words.ex: Sustainable energy means using the sun and wind — not stealing your neighbour's electricity.
frames: This approach is not sustainable because ___.

words.def: the purpose something is designed for, or to work correctly
words.ex: The function of a alarm clock is to wake you up. Its actual function is to be hit and ignored.
frames: The main function of ___ is to ___.
This serves an important function in ___.

words.def: last; coming at the end
words.ex: The final exam is your last chance to prove you learned something — or your last chance to guess creatively.
frames: Finally, it should be noted that ___.

words.def: the ideas, customs, and way of life of a group of people
words.ex: Every culture has its own food traditions — in some cultures, eating insects is normal; in others, eating pineapple on pizza is the real crime.
frames: ___ plays an important role in ___ culture.

words.def: without being affected by; even though
words.ex: Despite studying all night, he still fell asleep during the exam. At least he studied in his dreams.

words.def: happening or existing before the one being talked about
words.ex: Previous research suggested that goldfish have short memories. Previous goldfish could not be reached for comment.

words.def: an organisation set up for a special purpose, especially research or education
words.ex: The institute published a study proving that people who read studies are 100% more likely to quote them at dinner.
frames: Educational institutions play a key role in ___.

words.def: to judge or calculate the amount, value, or size of something roughly
words.ex: Scientists estimate the universe is 13.8 billion years old. They estimate my maths homework will take forever.
frames: The total cost was estimated at ___.

words.def: a piece of written or printed material, especially a book or document
words.ex: The text was so long that by the time I finished reading it, I forgot what the first page said.
frames: The main idea of this text is ___.

words.def: to produce a change in someone or something; to influence
words.ex: The weather affects your mood — rain makes you sad, sun makes you happy, and exams make you question your life choices.
frames: The changes are likely to affect ___.

words.def: very important and basic; forming the foundation of something
words.ex: Understanding grammar is fundamental to learning English — understanding memes is fundamental to surviving the internet.
frames: This represents a fundamental change in ___.
One fundamental difference between ___ and ___ is ___.

words.def: a particular place or position
words.ex: The location of the restaurant was perfect — right next to a gym, so you could feel guilty while eating.
frames: The geographical location of ___ makes it ___.

words.def: being the only one of its kind; very special or unusual
words.ex: Every student is unique — uniquely talented at finding excuses for late homework.
frames: This provides a unique opportunity to ___.

words.def: relating to medicine or the treatment of illness
words.ex: Medical advances mean people live longer — which means more years of being told to eat vegetables.
frames: Advances in medical research have led to ___.
Patients should seek medical advice before ___.
Access to medical care is essential for ___.

words.def: happening at the end of a process or period of time
words.ex: Eventually, the student finished the essay — only three days after the deadline.

words.def: easy to see or understand; clear
words.ex: It was obvious that he had not studied — his answer to every question was "I agree with the person next to me."
frames: The most obvious difference between ___ and ___ is ___.

words.def: one part or feature of a situation, plan, or subject
words.ex: The most important aspect of cooking is not burning the kitchen down. Everything else is optional.
frames: This essay will examine various aspects of ___.
Another key aspect to consider is ___.

words.def: an example or single occurrence of something
words.ex: In this instance, the student was right and the teacher was wrong — a rare instance indeed.

words.def: a discussion in which people express different opinions about a topic
words.ex: The debate about whether homework is useful has been going on for years — students already know the answer.
frames: There is an ongoing debate about whether ___.
This has sparked a debate about ___.

words.def: a statement of the exact meaning of a word, or the act of making something clear
words.ex: By definition, a dictionary defines words. By practice, students use it to press flowers.
frames: There is no single definition of ___.

words.def: happening at the beginning; first
words.ex: My initial reaction to the exam was panic. My second reaction was also panic.

words.def: something useful that is available to use, especially money, materials, or staff
words.ex: Natural resources are limited — but your teacher's patience is an even more limited resource.
frames: The use of natural resources must be ___.

words.def: to make information available to the public, usually in a book, article, or online
words.ex: The study was published in a top journal — which means about twelve people read it.
frames: A recently published study found that ___.

words.def: a connection or relationship between two things
words.ex: Researchers found a link between eating chocolate and happiness — finally, some useful science.
frames: There is a clear link between ___ and ___.
The link between ___ and ___ is well established.

words.def: to appear or come out; to become known
words.ex: A new trend has emerged among students: studying. Just kidding — it's still social media.
frames: A new trend has emerged in ___.
___ has emerged as a key factor in ___.

words.def: the tools, machines, or things needed for a particular activity
words.ex: The laboratory had the latest equipment — unfortunately, it did not come with instructions.
frames: A lack of equipment means that ___.

words.def: exact and accurate; very careful about details
words.ex: The scientist was very precise — she measured everything to the millimetre, except her coffee, which she poured by feeling.
frames: It is difficult to be precise about ___.
The precise nature of ___ remains unclear.

words.def: a goal or aim; or based on facts rather than personal feelings
words.ex: The main objective of the lesson was to learn grammar. The students' objective was to survive until lunch.
frames: The main objective of ___ is to ___.
In order to achieve this objective, ___.
It is important to remain objective when ___.

words.def: a number of similar things or events that come one after another
words.ex: The teacher asked a series of questions. The students gave a series of blank stares.
frames: The study involved a series of ___.
This was followed by a series of ___.

words.def: the ability to hold, contain, or produce something; or the maximum amount possible
words.ex: The stadium has a capacity of 50,000 people — or 100,000 if you count the ones standing on each other's shoulders.
frames: The ___ is operating at full capacity.

words.def: to change something completely, usually in a good way
words.ex: The internet has transformed how we learn — now we can be confused by millions of sources instead of just one textbook.
frames: Technology has transformed the way we ___.
This led to a major transformation in ___.
The ___ has been completely transformed by ___.

words.def: to show something to people, or a presentation of things for people to look at
words.ex: The museum displayed ancient pottery. The students displayed ancient boredom.
frames: The exhibit was on display at ___.

words.def: the situation or background in which something happens that helps explain it
words.ex: In the context of an exam, silence is golden. In the context of a party, silence is awkward.
frames: This must be understood in the context of ___.

words.def: to encourage or support something so it happens or develops
words.ex: Schools promote healthy eating — while selling chocolate bars in the cafeteria.
frames: This programme is designed to promote ___.
Efforts to promote ___ have resulted in ___.

words.def: to reach a decision or opinion after thinking about something; to end
words.ex: The researchers concluded that sleep is important. They reached this conclusion after staying awake for three days.
frames: It is reasonable to conclude that ___.

words.def: happening all the time or not changing
words.ex: The only constant thing in life is change — and the constant sound of your phone buzzing.
frames: The temperature must be kept constant at ___.

words.def: the act of using something up, especially food, energy, or resources
words.ex: Global energy consumption keeps rising — mostly because nobody remembers to turn off the lights.
frames: In order to reduce consumption of ___, ___.
Consumers are becoming more aware of ___.

words.def: a clear difference between two things when they are compared
words.ex: In contrast to the teacher's expectations, the students did very well — shocking everyone, including the students.
frames: There is a sharp contrast between ___ and ___.

words.def: connected to what is being discussed or considered; important to the matter
words.ex: The question asked about geography, but his answer about football was not exactly relevant.

words.def: to pay no attention to something or someone on purpose
words.ex: You can ignore advice, ignore warnings, and ignore your alarm clock — but you cannot ignore the consequences.
frames: It would be wrong to ignore ___.
This issue has been largely ignored by ___.
We cannot ignore the fact that ___.

words.def: a plan or set of rules agreed by a government or organisation
words.ex: The school introduced a new policy on phones — students must now be more creative about hiding them.
frames: The government should introduce policies to ___.
Changes in policy have led to ___.

words.def: clearly different or separate from other things
words.ex: There is a distinct difference between being quiet and being asleep in class — though both look the same.
frames: There is a distinct difference between ___ and ___.
Two distinct types of ___ can be identified.
The distinction between ___ and ___ is important because ___.

words.def: close to an exact amount or number but not completely accurate
words.ex: The journey takes approximately two hours — approximately meaning "definitely longer if you stop for snacks."
frames: This figure is only an approximation of ___.

words.def: extremely large in size, amount, or degree
words.ex: The exam covered an enormous amount of material — approximately everything ever written.
frames: This has had an enormous impact on ___.
An enormous amount of ___ is needed to ___.
